inlineContent.text: A theme can be copied, tweaked, styled, etc. This struct
it share copy logic with its style variation.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks a bunch of predefined themes and
style variations, as well as a theme-based style service.

abstract.text: This struct defines a keyboard-specific theme, which can
be used to define a bunch of styles at once.
Create a copy of the theme, without its author.
This protocol is implemented by pro theme components, to
make it possible to create mutable copies.
Get all predefined themes in their basic form.
The callout style to apply, if any.
This enum defines various button types.
Whether or not the theme is predefined.
The style to apply to autocomplete toolbars, if any.

vertical list, with horizontally scrolling shelves.

This article describes the KeyboardKit Pro theme engine, which can be used to style the entire keyboard with a single theme.
This protocol can be implemented by theme-specific style
variations, to ensure that a style creates unique copies.

KeyboardKit helps you create custom keyboard extensions with Swift and SwiftUI.
The Swifty theme only uses button backgrounds for input
keys and fades out a light tint color to clear.
